title: Case study — Rally HQ (paired with blueprint.rallyhq.app)
summary: Tournament management platform paired with a separate blueprint portal at blueprint.rallyhq.app — the canonical blueprint v2 portal deploy that this v3 redesign replicates for blueprint.ninochavez.co.

strategy.decision: Hero deep-dive for the volleyball-630 cluster. Five compositions: hero (with TWO live URL pills — rallyhq.app product + blueprint.rallyhq.app methodology) → schematic (rally-hq-blueprint-pipeline: the paired-deploy diagram) → approach (paired-deploy pattern + portal as design oracle) → quotable (the methodology applied) → outcome (live product + live methodology surface).
strategy.why: ADR-0009 cluster IA lists rally-hq as the hero for volleyball-630. It's the canonical reference for ADR-0008 (this site's paired-deploy adoption is modeled on rally-hq's). The two-surface presentation directly demonstrates the pattern this v3 redesign IS implementing — meta-reference works as positioning proof.
strategy.shipped: v2 /work/rally-hq — single live URL surfaced (rallyhq.app), blueprint surface (blueprint.rallyhq.app) mentioned in prose but not surfaced as a co-equal deploy.
strategy.gap: Proposed = TWO co-equal URL pills + paired-deploy schematic + meta-reference to v3's adoption of the same pattern; Shipped = single URL + prose mention of blueprint.
strategy.question: Does the meta-reference ('we built v3 using this same pattern') strengthen the case study or read as self-aggrandizing? Voice-guide check.

currentState.summary: v2 case page + single rallyhq.app link.
currentState.annotation: Route nests under volleyball-630. Surface BOTH live URLs (product + blueprint) as co-equal. Add paired-deploy schematic that doubles as the meta-reference to v3's pattern adoption.
